name: Snap
summary: A Snap is a custom grip used by SketchUp's Move tool. Snaps can be added at strategic places such as connectors to help assembling objects. #direction is the direction a snap is “pointing”. This can be thought of as the normal direction of the snap. It can also be thought of as the direction you move an object when plugging it into another object, e.g. inserting a power coord. #up controls the rotation around the Snap's axis. When two objects are snapped together, the Snaps have opposite #direction vectors but matching #up vectors.

methods.name: direction
methods.summary: The #direction method is used to get the direction this Snap is “pointing”.
methods.remarks: The #direction method is used to get the direction this Snap is “pointing”. When two Snaps are snapped into each other, they have the opposite #direction.

methods.name: position
methods.summary: The #position method is used to get the position of this Snap.
methods.remarks: The #position method is used to get the position of this Snap.

methods.name: set
methods.signature: set(position) => Sketchup::Snap | set(position, direction) => Sketchup::Snap | set(position, direction, up) => Sketchup::Snap
methods.summary: The #set method is used to move and/or reorient a Snap.
methods.remarks: The #set method is used to move and/or reorient a Snap.

methods.name: up
methods.summary: The #up method is used to get a vector representing the rotation of this Snap along its axis.
methods.remarks: The #up method is used to get a vector representing the rotation of this Snap along its axis. When two Snaps are snapped into each other, they have the same aligned #up direction.
